accept: There exists one canonical doc location for the error-envelope contract (act help errors, or a section in act help workflow).
Doc states: shape is {error, message, details?}; code categories include bad_flag, file_not_found, id_ambiguous, etc.; lengths are byte-counted unless explicitly noted.
CLAUDE.md or act help overview points new agents at this doc.
An agent picking up an unrelated issue can implement an error path without reading internal/cli/errors.go to figure out the contract.
payload.description: Sub-agent on act-6bbd reported: 're-deriving the error-envelope shape from existing code is the largest fixed cost in a single-issue cycle.' Specifically the {error, message, details?} shape used by emitEnvelope, the 'bad_flag' vs 'file_not_found'-style code distinction, and the byte-vs-rune length-validation precedent (per internal/op/payloads.go). New issues frequently touch error paths; documenting the contract once eliminates the detour. Suggested home: act help <topic> (e.g. 'act help errors' or fold into 'act help workflow'). CLAUDE.md should reference it. Either is acceptable; pick whichever is closer to where an agent will look first.
payload.title: act help / CLAUDE.md should describe the error-envelope contract
